title: potentilla pensylvanica var. litoralis
intro: Potentilla pensylvanica var. litoralis, also known as seaside cinquefoil, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the rose family and is native to the coastal regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection. The health benefits of Potentilla pensylvanica var. litoralis include its ability to reduce swelling, fight off bacterial and fungal infections, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions and respiratory problems.
